evidence.claim_boundary: Benchmark compares identity-lane string construction vs legacy narrative replay in Rust.

protocol.alternative_hypothesis: Hard-anchor construction omits heavy narrative payloads and keeps a large speedup over legacy replay.
protocol.null_hypothesis: Hard-anchor context construction has no meaningful speed or size advantage over legacy narrative replay.

required_fields.claim_boundary: Hard-anchor construction omits heavy narrative payloads, achieving a 25x speedup over legacy replay.
required_fields.decision: The measured speedup of 25.04x and compression ratio of 0.011 confirm that hard-anchors are significantly faster and smaller than legacy narrative replay. The legacy method processes ~41.5M chars with median latency 68.4ms, while hard-anchors process ~460k chars with median latency 2.73ms. The null hypothesis is rejected.
risks: Benchmark scope limited to identity-lane string construction; generalization to other payload types unverified.
Small sample size (7 repeats) may not capture tail latency outliers in production environments.
Memory overhead of anchor index structures not explicitly measured in this benchmark.

evidence_checks: Confirmed compression ratio 0.01107518 from context char reduction 459999/41534215
Validated timing measurements against raw nanosecond data
Checked anchor vs legacy payload size differential (460k vs 41.5M chars)
failure_modes: Benchmark limited to identity-lane construction only
Small sample size may miss production tail latencies
json.rationale: Solver correctly used hard measurement data showing 25x speedup and 99% compression. No evidence of lossy summary overreading. Risks appropriately identified.
